title: [FIRRTL] infer width (tail) 0-width
body:
The following FIRRTL program:

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    output tmp39: UInt<1>
    tmp39 <= tail(UInt("b1100000011001010"), 16)
```

Compiled with `firtool a_top_mod.fir --lower-to-hw --expand-whens --infer-widths --lowering-options=noAlwaysFF --verilog -o=a_top_mod_new.v` fails with the following error:

```
a_top_mod.fir:4:14: error: 'firrtl.tail' op result type should be '!firrtl.uint<0>'
    tmp39 <= tail(UInt("b1100000011001010"), 16)
             ^
a_top_mod.fir:4:14: note: see current operation: %0 = "firrtl.tail"(%c49354_ui16) {amount = 16 : i32} : (!firrtl.uint<16>) -> !firrtl.uint
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  output  tmp39
);
  assign tmp39 = 1'h0;
endmodule
```
